old_claim.note: Animal Husbandry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No redundant direct husbandry edge remains because the required animal_drawn_plows node already encapsulates the managed traction-animal pathway for the seeder-plow architecture.

source_shape.source_locator: Page 178 treats oxen and the seeder-plow together as one plowing team and defines the funnel as a modification of the plow.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The documented immediate machine dependency is the animal-drawn plow rather than a separate parallel husbandry component.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping both levels would duplicate a transitive livestock requirement and obscure the implement architecture that directly distinguishes this node.

ontology_before: The broad node listed both the immediate traction-plow system and its upstream animal-management dependency in parallel.
ontology_after: The seeder-plow links to its immediate mechanical base and relies on that node's own animal prerequisites transitively.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: heavy_draft_plowing no longer directly depends on animal_husbandry.
would_reject_if: The graph's animal_drawn_plows node ceased to represent managed draft animals or the seeder-plow used livestock independently of that plow system.
short_reason: Animal husbandry is already represented through the required animal-drawn plow node.
